title: Rytidosperma richardsonii
taxonomy: Rytidosperma richardsonii is classified as a species within the genus Rytidosperma and the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Danthonioideae and is a type of grass. The classification at the kingdom level places it as a Plantae, and at the phylum level as a Angiospermophyta. Within the order, it is classified as Poales and at the class level as Monocotyledonae. It is also known as the 'Purple Blue Grass'.
uses: Rytidosperma richardsonii, also known as curly wind grass, has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and wounds. It is also said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe and calm irritated skin. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits. As with any plant-based remedy, caution should be taken when using Rytidosperma richardsonii, and it's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before use.

parts: Rytidosperma richardsonii, commonly known as golden oat grass or Richardson's button-grass, is a species of flowering plant native to Australia.
